channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Solarpunk has got a release date, coming June 8th
item.content:encoded:
After having to delay Solarpunk to this year, developer Cyberwave has confirmed that its survival game Solarpunk will launch on June 8th for PC, PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Switch. The PC version is being handled by Cyberwave while the console port of Solarpunk is being handled by Mi’pu’mi Games, a team that has worked on titles like Alan Wake 2, Indiana Jones & The Great Circle, and Anno 1800 console edition.

In a blog post Cyberwave said, “During the Triple I Showcase, we officially revealed our release date, and after four years of development, this moment feels incredibly special to us. For most of that journey, Solarpunk was built by just the two of us, step by step, with a lot of passion, persistence, and probably more coffee than we should admit. As many of you know, the release has been delayed more than once along the way. Game development is rarely a straight line, especially for a small team, and bringing Solarpunk to the finish line took more time than we originally hoped. But getting here now makes this moment even more meaningful.”

In Solarpunk, players inhabit a world filled with floating islands filled with resources and opportunities to build. You can go solo or play with others to create environments in which your characters can live, using the sun, water, and wind as energy sources. You can build structures that can automate various processes including gathering resources and watering your plants, giving you more time to jump on your airship and see what the other floating islands have to offer.

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/10/solarpunk-has-got-a-release-date-coming-june-8th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Risk of Rain 2’s next DLC is Hallowed Concepts
item.content:encoded:
Gearbox has revealed that Risk of Rain 2 is getting more DLC, and this one is called Hallowed Concepts. There is no release date yet for Risk of Rain 2 Hallowed Concepts but an announcement trailer gives a look at brief glimpses of gameplay, as well as setting the tone of an eerie and haunting new location to discover.

Risk of Rain 2 Hallowed Concepts will see players dive deeper into the world of N’Kuhana, specifically going to the region known as Petrichor V. In this region players will be facing off against new enemy types, there will be curses to break, and deadly trials to overcome. Among the locations within Petrichor V are the Damp Caverns, Tenebrous Quagmire village which is home to the Lynx Tribe, and  The Lurid Basilica which is a major gathering point. There will be two new survivors to play as when Risk of Rain Hallowed Concepts arrives, and you can wishlist it on Steam now.

Source: Risk of Rain 2: Hallowed Concepts – Announcement Trailer



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/10/risk-of-rain-2s-next-dlc-is-hallowed-concepts/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: What We Played – People of Note, Starfield & The Occultist
item.content:encoded:
It’s been the Easter holidays around our house, so sleepy kids and days out have been the order of the day, with a smidgen of gaming here and there when everyone is recovering from being out in the fresh air. I had a fun time with People of Note this week, a music-based RPG that gives a nod to turn-based classics of old, while adding a touch of High School Musical/The Greatest Showman to proceedings.

Aran has been playing Hades 2, saying, “It has really got its claws into me. Just really having fun with it. I’ve also started Dosa Divas for review. That will be out next week!”

Good things come to those who wait, and in Gamoc’s case, that’s Starfield on PS5, which he’s busy putting together a review for. Alongside that he’s played Icarus, Ghost Master Resurrected, and some more Crimson Desert. He also played Resi Requiem, but it’s slow going as he told us, “I have to take breaks because I’m a coward.”

Nic B plat’d Requiem, and told us, “It was a blast. Infinite Requiem bullets are great fun. After that, I moved on to Spider-Man 2, which I’m playing with my wife in the evenings. It’s a stunning game to be sure.”

Steve has been on a welcome investigative horror ride with both The Occultist and Cthulhu: Dark Abyss. He told us, “I really enjoyed the first one and there is a lot to like about the second but it has a few non-intuitive aspects that might make it a chore if you don’t gel with it. I also got to play the excellent Subversive Memories – an indie survival horror about the Brazilian dictatorship that borrows equally from Signalis and Alan Wake. I’ve continued exploring the mansion of Blue Prince with my 14 year old and got agonisingly close to unlocking the last room a couple of times.”

It’s been a return to Life Is Strange – a reunion, if you will – for Tuffcub, who enjoyed returning for Max and Chloe’s final story together, even if it’s an ageing game format now.

Ade has really got into Khazan: The First Berserker. He said, “Souls games normally don’t appeal to me, but Khazan’s gorgeous anime characters caught my eye. Completely satisfying combat, awesome bosses, and a suprisingly doable difficulty (played on normal at least!) means that Khazan has won me over.”

And finally, Tef has largely come to the end of The Division: Resurgence, finding the final third of the story a real slog with an very flat ending… but he’s still dipping in each day to keep boosting his character’s stats and gear. Alongside that, he’s played the rogulite minotaur Dungeon Keeper-alike Minos, and you can expect a review of that rather soon.

How about you? What have you played?



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/10/what-we-played-people-of-note-starfield-the-occultist/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A Crimson Desert update will add control remaps, new content, and more
item.content:encoded:
My social media feed seems to be endless Crimson Desert videos, mostly involving tubby cats, and it has almost convinced me to buy the game. However, the very odd control scheme has been putting me off, so it is good news that a new update will allow some customisation.

Here’s an overview of what will be coming in the update, which currently is undated.

New Content and Features
The upcoming updates will introduce additional content aimed at players seeking additional challenges:

  • Boss Rematch and Re-blockading: Players will be able to re-challenge previously defeated bosses and liberated locations may come under blockade again, providing players more opportunities for battle.
  • Difficulty Settings: Easy, normal, and hard difficulty options will allow players to adjust combat difficulty for their preferences.

Character and Gameplay Improvements
Enhancements are also planned to improve character balance and immersion:

  • New Skills: Additional abilities will be introduced for characters Damiane and Oongka, aligning them more closely with the abilities of Kliff.
  • Back Weapon Visibility Toggle: Players will be able to hide back-mounted weapons to maintain visual immersion during gameplay.
  • Outfit Update: New character outfits will be introduced. Additionally, certain outfits previously limited to sale or donation will be updated to become equippable.

Expanded Systems and Quality-of-Life Updates
Several convenience features and system expansions are in development:

  • Specialized Storage Systems:
  • Food storage with direct access to cooking ingredients
  • Wardrobe system for outfit and armor organisation
  • Gatherables storage for materials such as insects, stones, and ores
  • Collections storage for quest items, recipes, etc.
  • Pets and Mounts: New pets and summonable mounts will be added, along with expanded equipment options for mounts apart from horses

Technical and Visual Enhancements
The development team is also working on broader system and graphical improvements:

  • UI Updates: Various UI elements improvements, including adjustable font sizes
  • Control Improvements: Additional customization options for certain controller inputs and expanded configurable keys for mouse and keyboard controls.
  • Improvements to Distant Scenery Quality: Improvements to the rendering of distant landscapes to enhance immersion.

Crimson Desert Original Soundtrack
A free soundtrack DLC will be released on Steam, with the official Crimson Desert soundtrack also becoming available on major global music platforms.

Source: Press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/10/crimson-desert-update-control-remaps-new-content/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Don’t Starve Elsewhere promises a new world of foggy horrors to survive
item.content:encoded:
Klei Entertainment has revealed Don’t Starve Elsewhere, an all-new sequel that promises to ramp up the “uncompromising survival experience” and really put multiplayer and co-op front and centre.

After over a decade of familiarity with the original, not to mention its expanded co-op Don’t Starve Together release, Don’t Starve Elsewhere looks to put down fresh foundations. There’s the same hand drawn art style throughout, but there’s less of the paper cutout vibe and the world is now built in 3D – I think this is new? It’s been a long, long time.

Get your friends together, craft a home base, then head out to gather resources. You can venture to snowy mountains, cross rivers and seas, and delve into the new cave systems. Each biome has its own dangers, as well as its own resources to draw you there, and its own particular weather patterns  and climates. And always there’s the Fog, which moves across the landscape and can hide its own alluring secrets, if you wish to risk its madness.

Don’t Starve Elsewhere is announced for PC and is currently available to wishlist on Steam.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/09/dont-starve-elsewhere-promises-a-new-world-of-foggy-horrors-to-survive/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
